title: juncus uliginosus var. subverticillatus
intro: Juncus uliginosus var. subverticillatus, also known as the marsh rush, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Juncaceae and is native to parts of Europe, Asia, and North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The marsh rush is believed to have health benefits such as reducing swelling, soothing skin irritations, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
